docs.title: Deploy process review 2026-05-03
docs.content:
Deploy Process Review 2026-05-03

Participants: ivan, jess, kiran

ivan: We need to revisit the Fly deployment runbook. The current doc on Notion doesn't cover IPv6 binding for 6PN calls.
jess: Right, the Notion 'Fly Deployment Guide' page says to bind on 0.0.0.0 but that breaks internal service calls over 6PN.
kiran: I updated the page yesterday. We need to bind on :: for 6PN and use 0.0.0.0 for the health check endpoint.
ivan: Can someone verify the Notion doc reflects both bind addresses now?
jess: I'll do a pass after this meeting.

docs.title: Fly Deployment Guide
docs.content:
Fly Deployment Guide

This guide covers deploying services to Fly.io.

## Network binding
- Bind your HTTP server on '::' (dual-stack IPv6) for internal 6PN calls between Fly apps
- For health check endpoints, bind uvicorn on '0.0.0.0' (IPv4) since Fly health probes are IPv4-only

## fly.toml tips
- Set [[vm]] count, then follow up with flyctl scale count N
- CI deploys automatically on push to main

expected_edges.note: Meeting explicitly references and discusses the Notion Fly Deployment Guide page's IPv6 binding guidance
